Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, which may indicate possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology. Urine is brown, cloudy, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low specific gravity may reflect impaired concentrating ability. Hemoglobin A1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss/transfusion. Recommend further renal and metabolic evaluation and clinical correlation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
